landscapeTheme: UCF Headquarters â€” reflection, wrap-up, return to base

scenes.heading: Your Takeaway Resources from Campus HQ
scenes.narration: With your readiness assessment complete, think of this as walking through the UCF BIP resource center before heading out. You would not leave campus headquarters without picking up the materials that turn everything you learned into a real plan. This lesson covers four downloadable resources and your digital credential. Each resource serves a different purpose. The checklist keeps you organized. The roadmap keeps you in sequence. The resource guide connects you to help. And the decision worksheet keeps you honest about readiness. Download all four now. They pair directly with your one-on-one meeting with UCF BIP. Let me walk you through each one.

scenes.heading: The Checklist and the Roadmap
bullets: U.S. Market Entry Checklist: module-by-module action items with priority levels and timeframes
Start with the ecosystem section â€” it connects you with free resources first
Florida Setup Roadmap: step-by-step sequence from pre-arrival through operational readiness
Many founders complete phases one and two remotely, then open a bank account in one visit
scenes.narration: Resource one is the U.S. Market Entry Checklist. Think of it as your pre-launch checklist â€” every system verified. It turns every major topic from this program into action items grouped by module: legal setup, finances, IP protection, team building, market entry, culture, and ecosystem resources. Each item includes a priority level and suggested timeframe. Start with the ecosystem section â€” it connects you with free resources before you start paying for services. Resource two is the Florida Setup Roadmap. This is your launch sequence â€” what fires first, second, third. It walks through four phases: pre-arrival preparation that you can do remotely, entity formation and registration, financial infrastructure, and operational readiness. Many founders complete the first two phases remotely before arriving so they can open a bank account in a single visit with all documents ready.

scenes.heading: The Resource Guide and the Decision Worksheet
bullets: Central Florida Resource Guide: directory of organizations, contacts, and programs in one place
Covers SBDC, SCORE, UCF BIP, chambers, attorneys, CPAs, and banking contacts
Expansion Decision Worksheet: structured go/no-go framework across five areas
Not pass/fail â€” it tells you which gap to close first
scenes.narration: Resource three is the Central Florida Resource Guide â€” your mission control directory. It puts all the support organizations from Module 7 into a single searchable reference: business advising through SBDC and SCORE, incubators and accelerators, government programs, professional services including attorneys, CPAs, and banking contacts, networking organizations, and UCF BIP phases two and three details. Bookmark it and update it as you discover new resources through networking. Resource four is the Expansion Decision Worksheet. This is your go/no-go decision tool. It scores you across five areas: market fit, financial readiness, legal readiness, operations, and timing. This is not pass-fail. A founder from Colombia scored two out of five on financial readiness, spent four months strengthening home-market revenue, re-scored at four, and entered the U.S. with a much stronger foundation. The worksheet tells you which gap to close first.

scenes.heading: Your Digital Credential
bullets: A verified digital badge for your LinkedIn profile, website, or email signature
Issued through POK Tech â€” the same provider used by Arizona State University
Program completion credential â€” signals structured, university-backed preparation
Claim process takes five minutes and the credential does not expire
scenes.narration: When you complete this program, you earn a digital credential â€” a verified badge for your LinkedIn profile, website, or email signature. It validates completion of the UCF BIP U.S. Market Readiness Program, backed by one of the largest and most active university incubation ecosystems in the country. The credential is issued through POK Tech, the same provider used by Arizona State University and other major universities. It is a program completion credential, not a professional certification â€” but it signals to everyone in your network that you took a structured, university-backed program seriously. The claim process takes about five minutes. You receive an email from POK Tech, set up your account, accept the credential, and share it to LinkedIn using the built-in tool. The credential does not expire.

scenes.heading: How These Resources Work Together
bullets: Complete the Decision Worksheet first to assess your overall readiness
Use the Checklist to identify specific action items by topic area
Follow the Roadmap to execute in the right sequence
Reference the Resource Guide to find the right professional or organization for each step
scenes.narration: Here is how these four resources work together. Start with the Expansion Decision Worksheet â€” it gives you the big picture of whether now is the right time and what gaps need closing. Then use the Market Entry Checklist to identify specific action items across all seven topic areas. Follow the Florida Setup Roadmap to execute those actions in the right sequence â€” what to do first, second, third. And reference the Central Florida Resource Guide whenever you need to find the right professional or organization for a specific step. Complete the Decision Worksheet before your one-on-one meeting with UCF BIP. It helps the team give you targeted advice based on your specific readiness profile rather than generic recommendations.

scenes.heading: Do Not Leave the Launchpad Without Your Equipment
scenes.narration: Do not leave campus headquarters without your equipment. These four resources are yours to keep, and they extend the value of this program beyond the screen and into your actual business planning. The checklist keeps you organized. The roadmap keeps you in sequence. The resource guide connects you to help. The decision worksheet keeps you honest. Download all four now and bring them to your one-on-one meeting with the UCF BIP team. And claim your digital credential on LinkedIn. It is a small step, but it signals to everyone in your network that you are serious about doing this the right way. Final lesson: your next steps â€” your UCF BIP pathway and the three actions that matter most in the next thirty days.
